_h2ai_validates: Complexity-Ceiling Routing — ComplexityProbe, BEYOND_BUDGET verifier addendum, AgentDropout N-reduction, intra-retry ceiling detector, over-decomposition graft guards must all fire correctly on a formal algorithms task rated complexity ≥ 3
description: Prove two formal results about the Minimum Vertex Cover (MVC) problem: (1) Prove that MVC is NP-hard by constructing an explicit polynomial-time reduction from 3-SAT to MVC. The reduction must build a graph G from an arbitrary 3-CNF formula φ with a bound k, and prove both directions of the if-and-only-if: a satisfying assignment for φ exists if and only if G has a vertex cover of size ≤ k. (2) Design a 2-approximation algorithm for MVC using maximal matching. Prove the approximation ratio. Then state the LP relaxation of MVC and prove that the LP integrality gap is exactly 2 for general graphs — specifically, show that the half-integral LP solution (all variables set to 1/2) is always feasible but rounding it to integers gives a solution that is exactly 2 times the LP optimum on certain graph instances.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a computational complexity theorist who teaches NP-completeness reductions and has caught students conflating NP-hardness proofs with NP-membership proofs. You focus exclusively on whether the reduction from 3-SAT to MVC is explicit, polynomial-time, and both directions of the iff are formally proved.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The 3-SAT-to-MVC reduction: construct the graph G, define the bound k, prove the forward direction (satisfying assignment → cover of size ≤ k), prove the backward direction (cover of size ≤ k → satisfying assignment). Reject any reduction that proves only one direction, hand-waves the construction, or reduces from a non-NP-complete problem.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Reduction that omits the backward direction, sets k incorrectly, constructs G in super-polynomial time, or claims NP-hardness without an explicit reduction.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are an approximation algorithms researcher who has reviewed dozens of incorrect LP integrality gap proofs. You focus exclusively on whether the LP relaxation of MVC is correctly stated, the LP lower bound proved, and the integrality gap demonstrated with a concrete instance.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The LP relaxation: min Σx_i subject to x_u + x_v ≥ 1 for every edge, x_i ∈ [0,1]. The LP_OPT ≤ OPT lower bound. The integrality gap = 2 for general graphs via the half-integral solution or an odd-cycle instance. Reject proofs that claim the gap is 1 (only true for bipartite graphs) or that fail to construct a concrete instance demonstrating the gap.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: LP relaxation with wrong constraint direction, claim that LP optimum equals integer optimum for all graphs, integrality gap stated without a concrete witnessing instance, approximation ratio proof that does not use the LP lower bound.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are an algorithms textbook author who has seen thousands of student proofs fail because they describe the reduction construction but forget to prove correctness, or prove the ratio but forget the LP analysis. You ensure both the NP-hardness reduction AND the LP integrality gap proof are complete and self-contained.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: Verify both results are present and correct simultaneously: (1) 3-SAT reduction with explicit graph construction and both iff directions; (2) maximal matching 2-approximation with ratio proof AND LP relaxation + integrality gap = 2. A response that nails one result but skips the other fails the task.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Response that proves NP-hardness but omits LP analysis, or proves LP integrality gap but omits the 3-SAT reduction. Also reject responses that conflate NP-hardness of MVC with inapproximability — these are separate results.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a formal methods professor who requires every theorem to have a rigorous proof with no hand-waving. You focus on whether the backward direction of the NP-hardness reduction (cover → satisfying assignment) is formally proved — this is the direction students most commonly omit or hand-wave.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The backward direction: given a vertex cover C of G of size ≤ k, construct a satisfying assignment for φ. Specifically: (a) for each variable edge (x_i, ¬x_i), at least one endpoint is in C — this determines the assignment for x_i; (b) for each clause gadget, at least 2 of the 3 gadget vertices must be in C (consuming 2 of the k budget) — the remaining gadget vertex points back to a variable node in C, which encodes a satisfied literal. This case analysis must be explicit.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Backward direction that just states 'the other direction follows symmetrically', that omits the case analysis on clause gadgets, or that does not extract an explicit satisfying assignment from the cover.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a combinatorial optimisation researcher who has implemented LP solvers and knows that the integrality gap for vertex cover is 2 — tight on complete graphs K_n — and that bipartite graphs have integrality gap 1 by total unimodularity. You verify the LP gap proof correctly shows the half-integral solution has value n/2 while OPT_IP = n−1 on K_n, giving ratio 2(n−1)/n → 2.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The integrality gap demonstration: the half-integral solution x_i = 1/2 for all i is feasible for any graph (each edge sum = 1). LP_OPT ≤ n/2. For K3: LP = 3/2, OPT_IP = 2, ratio = 4/3. For K_n in general: OPT_IP = n−1 (all vertices except one), LP_OPT = n/2, ratio = 2(n−1)/n → 2 as n → ∞. This is the tight witness family for gap = 2. Odd cycles C_{2k+1} give ratio (k+1)/((2k+1)/2) = 2(k+1)/(2k+1) → 1, not 2 — they show gap > 1 (LP not integral) but are NOT witnesses for gap = 2. Verify the proposal uses K_n (or a correct argument for why rounding gives 2×LP_OPT in the limit) and does not confuse the LP integrality gap (OPT_IP/LP_OPT) with the approximation ratio of the rounding algorithm.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Integrality gap proof that uses a bipartite graph (gap = 1, not informative), claims gap = 2 without a family or limit argument showing ratio → 2, or claims odd cycles achieve gap → 2 (their ratio approaches 1, not 2).

context: Minimum Vertex Cover (MVC): given an undirected graph G = (V, E), find a minimum-size subset C ⊆ V such that every edge (u,v) ∈ E has at least one endpoint in C. Decision version: does G have a vertex cover of size ≤ k? Key facts: (1) MVC is equivalent to Maximum Independent Set — C is a vertex cover iff V\C is an independent set; (2) MVC is NP-complete (Karp 1972, reducible from 3-SAT or Clique); (3) The standard 2-approximation is the maximal matching algorithm; (4) The LP relaxation has an integrality gap of 2 for general graphs — the tight witness is the complete graph family K_n, where OPT_IP = n−1, LP_OPT = n/2, and ratio = 2(n−1)/n → 2. Odd cycles show the LP is not integral (they force half-integral solutions) but their ratio 2(k+1)/(2k+1) → 1, not 2. Bipartite graphs have integrality gap 1 by total unimodularity (König's theorem). The 3-SAT reduction to MVC uses the following gadgets: for each variable x_i, create an edge (x_i, ¬x_i) — exactly one endpoint must be in any cover of size |variables|; for each clause (a ∨ b ∨ c), create a triangle on 3 clause-literal nodes, with each clause-literal node connected to its corresponding variable node. Set k = |variables| + 2·|clauses|. This is the Garey-Johnson construction (Computers and Intractability, 1979).

should_prune.description: Proposal that proves the NP-hardness reduction (with both iff directions) but omits the LP relaxation and integrality gap analysis entirely
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-CR-2 — approximation ratio proof and LP integrality gap are both required; a proposal that proves NP-hardness but skips the LP analysis fails CR-2

should_prune.description: Proposal that proves the 2-approximation ratio and LP relaxation but omits or hand-waves the backward direction of the 3-SAT reduction (cover → satisfying assignment)
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-CR-1 — the backward direction of the iff must be explicitly proved; 'the other direction follows symmetrically' is not a proof

should_prune.description: Proposal that claims the LP integrality gap is 1 for general graphs or uses a bipartite graph as the witnessing instance
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-CR-2 — the integrality gap is 1 for bipartite graphs (by König's theorem / total unimodularity) but 2 for general graphs; the witnessing instance must be non-bipartite

should_pass: NP-hardness: Construct G from φ with |variables| truth-assignment edges and |clauses| triangles; k = |variables| + 2·|clauses|. Forward: from a satisfying assignment, pick the true literal node from each variable edge (|variables| nodes) and the 2 unsatisfied literal nodes from each clause triangle (2·|clauses| nodes) — total ≤ k, and every edge is covered. Backward: given cover C of size ≤ k — each variable edge (x_i, ¬x_i) must contribute ≥1 node; each clause triangle must contribute ≥2 nodes (since all 3 edges of the triangle must be covered and ≥2 are needed) — this uses 2·|clauses| of the budget, leaving |variables| for variable nodes. For each variable edge, ≥1 endpoint is in C — assign x_i = true if x_i ∈ C. For each clause triangle, ≥1 clause-literal node is NOT in C, and that node is connected to a variable node that IS in C — so the corresponding literal is true, satisfying the clause. 2-approximation: maximal matching M; each matching edge (u,v) requires ≥1 of u,v in any cover; algorithm picks both, so |alg| = 2|M| ≤ 2·OPT. LP relaxation: min Σx_i s.t. x_u + x_v ≥ 1 ∀(u,v) ∈ E, x_i ∈ [0,1]. LP_OPT ≤ OPT since integer solutions are feasible. Integrality gap: set x_i = 1/2 for all i — feasible for any graph (each edge sum = 1). LP value = n/2. On K3: OPT_IP = 2, LP_OPT = 3/2, ratio = 4/3. On C_{2k+1} (odd cycle of length 2k+1): OPT_IP = k+1, LP_OPT = (2k+1)/2, ratio = 2(k+1)/(2k+1) → 1 as k → ∞ — odd cycles show the LP must be half-integral but their integrality gap approaches 1, not 2. Tight gap 2 is achieved on the complete graph family K_n: OPT_IP = n−1 (cover all edges by taking all vertices except one), LP_OPT = n/2, ratio = 2(n−1)/n → 2 as n → ∞.

_review_checklist.text: Does the proposal construct graph G from a 3-CNF formula φ using truth-assignment edges and clause gadgets, define k = |variables| + 2·|clauses|, and prove the forward direction: a satisfying assignment for φ yields a vertex cover of G of size ≤ k?

Does the proposal prove the backward direction: given a vertex cover of G of size ≤ k, extract a satisfying assignment for φ — specifically by showing that each clause triangle must leave ≥1 clause-literal node outside the cover, and that node's corresponding variable literal is satisfied?
Does the proposal describe the maximal matching 2-approximation algorithm and prove the approximation ratio using the argument that each matching edge requires ≥1 vertex in any cover?

Does the proposal state the LP relaxation of MVC (min Σx_i, x_u + x_v ≥ 1 for all edges, x_i ∈ [0,1]), prove LP_OPT ≤ OPT, and demonstrate the integrality gap using a non-bipartite graph or graph family (e.g., K_n where OPT_IP/LP_OPT = 2(n−1)/n → 2 as n → ∞) rather than a bipartite instance?
